name: Muay ThAI
description: A two-player 3-distance Muay Thai game where agents secretly queue three actions, manage footwork, store short-term damage with predictions, and cash it with clean hits.
rules:
OVERVIEW
Two players fight a simultaneous three-action Muay Thai exchange. Both fighters are equal nak muay. Each starts at 30 HP, Distance range, with front feet on -1 and 1. Win by knockout or judges after 20 exchanges.

DISTANCES AND FEET
There are exactly three distances derived from the front-foot number line. Gap 0 is Clinch with overlapping front feet and lead hands. Gap 1 is toe-to-toe Pocket. Gap 2 is Distance, and the gap is capped there. P1 advance moves +1, P1 retreat moves -1 unless already capped at Distance. P2 advance moves -1, P2 retreat moves +1 unless already capped at Distance. A capped Distance retreat hop-steps out and returns to the same foot number unless the opponent advances with them. Fighters cannot pass through each other; advancing in Clinch clamps instead of side-switching.

ACTIONS
Legal movement actions are advance, retreat, and roll. Legal attacks are teep, flying_knee, jab, cross, elbow, and knee. Distance attacks: teep Light, flying_knee Heavy. Pocket attacks: jab Light, cross Heavy. Clinch attacks: elbow Light, knee Heavy. There is no line choice; every strike targets the head.

DAMAGE AND BONUSES
All attacks deal 2 base damage. Stored damage is earned after an action resolves, spends all at once on that fighter's next landed hit, carries through one following exchange, and expires if still unspent after that exchange. Newly earned stored damage never applies to the same action.

COMMITMENT TRIANGLE
Movement resolves before attacks. Light tags Movement: a Light attack that is in final range hits a moving target and gives the attacker +1 stored damage. Movement dodges Heavy: any movement makes a Heavy attack miss and gives the mover +2 stored damage. Heavy stuffs Light: when both are in range, Heavy lands, Light is stuffed, and the Heavy attacker gains +1 stored damage.

COMBOS AND PRESSURE
Landing hits in consecutive actions adds immediate combo damage: the second landed hit in a row adds +2 damage, and the third adds +4 damage. Any dodge increments a dodge streak. A second consecutive dodge grants +4 extra combo stored damage on top of any normal dodge storage. Advance against retreat keeps range stable and gives the advancer +1 stored damage, so pure retreat still has a cost.

ACTION FORMAT
Agents submit JSON like {"type":"queue","attack1":"jab","attack2":"cross","attack3":"advance","intent":"Jab to catch movement, threaten cross, then pressure the feet.","risk":"Movement can make heavy attacks miss.","trash_talk":"Hands up."}. Repeating the same action is allowed. Invalid actions become jab.
